chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-1585 of 2022 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE (S) 1. For orders on M.A. No.5640/2022. 2. For orders on office objections appearing on reverse of memo of petition. 3. For orders on M.A. No.5641/2022. 4. For hearing of main case. 21.04.2022 Mr. Sajjad Ali Gopang, Advocate for the petitioner. == 1. Urgency is granted. 2to4. Petitioner being an employee of University of Engineering, Science and Technology Nawabshah, has filed this petition to object the procurement of the equipment of the Lab. It is the case of the petitioner that in all fairness the University should have raised the construction of a building complete in all respects and then should have gone for the procurement. We have inquired initially as to how the petitioner is aggrieved of it, counsel has stated initially that his salary is being paid in installments therefore he has filed this petition that the salary be given first before procurement. We asked him that as to whether petitioner could initiate proceedings for recovery of his salary if being paid in installments, he declines and submits that he has not raised this ground in the memo of petition. We then asked him again as to how the petitioner is aggrieved of such procurement of equipment, counsel has failed to convince this Court that he being an employee of the University is aggrieved of such procurement of Lab equipment. Faced with situation, at this stage, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he would not press this petition and would act in accordance with law, so far as, the recovery of petitioner’s salary is concerned which is being paid in installments.

We then asked him again as to how the petitioner is aggrieved of such procurement of equipment, counsel has failed to convince this Court that he being an employee of the University is aggrieved of such procurement of Lab equipment. Faced with situation, at this stage, learned counsel for the petitioner submits that he would not press this petition and would act in accordance with law, so far as, the recovery of petitioner’s salary is concerned which is being paid in installments. Petitions stands dismissed as not pressed with pending application. JUDGE JUDGE Muhammad Danish*
